note: Source URL https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program resolves and is authentic official OSHA NRTL program page covering 29 CFR Part 1910.7. UL 61730 standard exists (Ed.1-2017 superseded by Ed.2-2022; Ed.3 of Part 2 published March 2026 adopting IEC 61730-2 Third Edition). NOTE: row text cites 'UL 61730 (Ed. 1, 2017; harmonised with IEC 61730-1/-2:2016)' which is now outdated — current active edition is Ed.2-2022 (Part 1 & Part 2), harmonising IEC 61730 Second Edition. The [UNVERIFIED] flag in the row text correctly surfaces this. NEC Article 690 and NRTL mandatory-status description are accurate. Gap description is factually sound.

Source URL https://www.cbp.gov/trade/forced-labor/UFLPA is a confirmed live official CBP page (WebFetch returned HTTP 403 due to server-side bot restriction, not a dead URL; direct search confirms page exists at that exact path). UFLPA Pub. L. 117-78 enacted 2021-12-23, effective 2022-06-21 — row states 'Pub. L. 117-78 (2021)' which is correct (enacted 2021, effective 2022-06-21). 19 U.S.C. 1307 citation correct. IRA Pub. L. 117-169 correct. IRC 48(a)(12) domestic-content ITC adder confirmed via IRS and Treasury guidance (Notice 2023-29 referenced in row; IRS has since issued Notice 2024-84 and Notice 2025-08). Adder is 2pp base / up to 10pp with prevailing-wage and apprenticeship — row says 'up to 10 percentage points' which is accurate for the maximum. Row [UNVERIFIED] caveats on adder percentages are appropriate given ongoing Treasury/IRS updates. Mandatory-status and gap descriptions are accurate.

SOURCE URL WRONG: https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?UniqueKey=34410 resolves to UL 1812 (Ducted Heat Recovery Ventilators), NOT UL 61730. URL has been updated to the correct UL 61730-1 product page (UniqueKey=43587, Edition 3, published 2022-10-28). ALSO NOTE: row text states 'UL 61730 (Ed. 1, 2017)' as the current governing standard — this is outdated. Current active editions are UL 61730-1 Ed.2-2022 and UL 61730-2 Ed.2-2022 (Part 2 Ed.3 published March 2026). The row's [UNVERIFIED] flag on edition alignment correctly flags this. Standards cited (UL 61730, UL 1703, 29 CFR Part 1910.7, IEC 61730) all exist and are accurate. UL 1703 withdrawal for new certifications is confirmed. Mandatory-status and gap descriptions are accurate.

SOURCE URL WRONG: https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/61637 returns HTTP 404. URL has been updated. Standards cited (IEC 61215-1:2021, IEC 61215-2:2021, IEC 62788) exist and are accurate. IEC 62788 is a real in-development series. IEC 61215 non-mandatory-but-effectively-required status is accurate. Perovskite damp-heat qualification barrier is well-documented in literature. Mandatory-status and gap descriptions are accurate.

SOURCE URL DEAD: https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-61730 returns HTTP 404. A live UL resource page on the UL 61730 / IEC 61730 harmonized standard exists at https://www.ul.com/resources/iec-ul-61730-new-harmonized-standard-safety-usa-and-international-access-one-standard. URL has been updated. Standards cited (UL 61730-1/-2, UL 790, ASTM E108, NEC Article 690) all exist and are accurate. UL 790 and ASTM E108 relationship (equivalent fire test protocols) is confirmed. Fire Classes A/B/C under UL 61730 via UL 790 tests confirmed by multiple sources. NEC Article 690 minimum Class C requirement confirmed. Mandatory-status description is accurate. Gap description is accurate.

Source URL https://www.epa.gov/hw/defining-hazardous-waste-listed-characteristic-and-mixed-radiological-wastes#characteristic resolves and is official EPA hazardous waste page confirming TCLP (SW-846 Method 1311) and toxicity characteristics. EPA's own solar FAQ page (https://www.epa.gov/hw/solar-panel-frequent-questions) independently confirms TCLP D008 lead threshold of 5.0 mg/L. RCRA Subtitle C (42 U.S.C. 6921 et seq.) exists. 40 CFR Part 261.24 D008 threshold correct. Peer-reviewed literature confirms lead-perovskite TCLP exceedance risk. EPA solar FAQ does NOT specifically mention perovskite modules (focuses on CdTe/Si), consistent with the row's [UNVERIFIED] flag. California AB 1210 and Washington RCW 70A.355 citations marked [UNVERIFIED] in row — appropriate caution. Mandatory-status ('mandatory if triggered') framing is legally accurate. Row is well-flagged as [UNVERIFIED] throughout and needs_human is appropriate.
